place: Mullica Hill CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Mullica Hill’s name comes from Swedish‑Finnish settlers Eric, John, Olag, and William Mullica who began buying land in 1704.
A railroad spur built in the 19th century turned the village into one of the nation’s busiest agricultural shipping points.
The town hall, still standing, was constructed after the Civil War and is a key example of post‑war architecture.
In 1991, the entire village was listed on the National Register of Historic Places for its architectural and commercial significance.
Every fall, Mullica Hill hosts a Civil War reenactment that celebrates the period of industrial and agricultural growth.
The area’s early Quaker plantations, established in the late 17th century, laid the groundwork for the town’s later development.
